pi_items.source: POE5 (Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition) dataset collection; verified via environment documentation at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/gretl_env/evidence_docs/README.md
Environment evidence documentation confirms usa.gdt contains variable 'gdp': Real GDP in billions of chained dollars, quarterly from FRED database (FRED series GDPC96)
Task description explicitly states creation of gdp_growth via log-difference method: ln(GDP_t) - ln(GDP_{t-1})
Task description explicitly requires: 'Generate a Periodogram (raw spectral density) for this new gdp_growth variable. Do NOT use a smoothed estimator'
Task description and export_result.sh script both specify this output path location
Task description and export_result.sh script both specify this output path location

privileged_info_summary: The usa.gdt dataset is a real, publicly-available dataset from the POE5 (Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition) collection, sourced from the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database. It contains quarterly macroeconomic data spanning 103 quarters from 1984Q1 to 2009Q3, including Real GDP (variable 'gdp' based on FRED series GDPC96) and inflation rate (variable 'inf'). The task requires creating a new variable 'gdp_growth' via log-difference transformation and generating a periodogram (raw spectral density) analysis without smoothing. Output files are saved to specified paths in /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/.
